strengths: Novel claim-level memory schema with explicit validity intervals and contradiction/supersede edges
Paired set-inclusion gains on abstention and SSU slices with rigorous statistical tests
Ablation isolates LLM linker as the causal mechanism for truth maintenance gains
Efficiency improvements over A-Mem with -62.1% token reduction
weaknesses: Overall accuracy not statistically distinguishable from Flat RAG (p=0.21)
Regression on assistant-authored, preference, and multi-session slices due to structural limitations
Theoretical analysis of Algorithm 1 lacks quantitative validation of the Δ-degree assumption
Router composition relies on zero-shot classifier with low recall on multi-session questions
required_revisions: Clarify the Δ-degree bound in Proposition 1 with empirical degree distribution from retrieval graphs
Quantify F1-F3 failure modes through targeted ablation (e.g., by augmenting assistant-side claims)
Report statistical significance of the 12.7-15pp gap between deployable routers and oracle
Justify the choice of k_keep=3 vs k=8 in retrieval through ablation on k_keep sensitivity

strengths: Clear focus on truth maintenance as a distinct challenge in agentic memory
Paired McNemar tests and bootstrap CIs for rigorous statistical analysis
Ablation isolates the LLM linker as the causal driver of gains
Efficiency comparison against A-Mem shows substantial token reduction
weaknesses: Overall accuracy not statistically distinguishable from Flat RAG (p=0.21)
Regressions on non-target slices (SSA, SSP, MS) with no mitigation strategy
Zero-shot router uses Kimi-K2 at 64.7% intent accuracy, creating a bottleneck
Stratified 150-question slice may not generalize to full LongMemEval-S
required_revisions: Report effect sizes and CIs for all McNemar tests, not just selected slices
Quantify the trade-off between abstention fidelity and recall loss on non-temporal slices
Provide error analysis for the 8/8 MS paired failures to validate F3's session-aggregation hypothesis
Clarify whether the 95% bootstrap CIs in Table 1 are overlapping due to shared reader model

strengths: Structured claim schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges addresses a key gap in existing memory systems
Paired McNemar tests on targeted slices (abstention, SSU) show strict set-inclusion gains over baselines
Ablation isolates the LLM linker as the causal mechanism for truth-maintenance gains
Efficiency metrics demonstrate -62.1% token reduction vs A-Mem while maintaining retrieval quality
weaknesses: Standalone TTMG regresses on assistant-authored (SSA) and multi-session (MS) slices due to structural limitations
Zero-shot routing via Kimi-K2 adds external dependencies not present in the core TTMG mechanism
Maximum-consistent-subgraph algorithm assumes pairwise contradictions but lacks handling for higher-order conflicts
Volatility classes and confidence scores rely on LLM writer calibration without explicit validation
required_revisions: Improve assistant-side claim extraction density to address F1 writer entity gap
Add multi-claim composition mechanisms for multi-session reasoning
Quantify calibration of confidence scores and volatility classes
Evaluate router alternatives that don't depend on external LLMs like Kimi-K2

strengths: Novel claim-level memory schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Strong empirical gains on abstention (+44.4 pp) and single-session-user (+9.5 pp) slices
Comprehensive ablation showing LLM linker is causal for all targeted improvements
Efficient: 62 % fewer tokens than A-Mem, no cross-encoder reranker needed
Full reproducibility package: code, prompts, seeds, per-question outputs released
weaknesses: Overall accuracy not statistically better than Flat RAG (61.3 % vs 68.0 %, p=0.21)
Evaluation on only 150 stratified questions; no full 500-question LongMemEval-S result
Intent classifier recall low on multi-session (42.5 %) and knowledge-update (39.1 %)
Writer under-extracts assistant-authored claims, causing systematic regression on SSA slice
No statistical correction for multiple slice-wise comparisons
required_revisions: Run and report full LongMem-Eval-S 500-question results with confidence intervals
Provide Bonferroni or FDR-adjusted p-values for the six slice-wise tests
Ablation that adds assistant-side claim density to close SSA gap without router
Error analysis on the 31 questions Flat RAG solves and TTMG misses beyond F1-F3 taxonomy
Clarify whether 0.0625 one-sided McNemar p=0.0625 is intended as significant (borderline)

strengths: Novel claim-level schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Paired strict-dominance gains on abstention (+44 pp) and single-session-user (+9.5 pp) slices
Thorough ablation showing the LLM linker is causally necessary (-22 pp on abstention)
Reproducible package: full prompts, code, seeds, per-question outputs, bootstrap CIs, McNemar tests
Lower token cost than A-Mem (-62 %) while adding structured truth maintenance
weaknesses: Only 9 abstention questions → McNemar p=0.0625 is marginal; no power analysis
Intent classifier recall 42.5 % on multi-session leaves 12–15 pp on the table vs oracle
Writer under-extracts assistant claims (7/7 SSA errors); no precision/recall reported for claim extraction
No statistical test for token/latency reduction; error bars missing on efficiency table
LongMemEval-S slice is 150/500 questions; no results on remaining 350 or other benchmarks (LoCoMo, MemoryAgentBench)
required_revisions: Add power calculation or increase abstention sample to firm up p=0.0625
Report claim-extraction precision/recall and assistant vs user density to quantify writer gap
Run full 500-question LongMemEval-S and at least one external benchmark to test generalisation
Provide confidence intervals or significance tests for token (-62 %) and latency (-27 %) reductions
Release the 350-question held-out intent-classifier data and confusion matrix for reproducibility

strengths: Claim-level schema with validity intervals and explicit contradict/supersede edges is a clean abstraction for truth maintenance
Session-batched writer + lexical gate keeps LLM linker off the hot path; real throughput win (-62 % tokens vs A-Mem)
Paired set-inclusion gains on abstention (+44 pp) and single-session-user (+9 pp) are measurable and backed by McNemar tests
Open-sourced prompts, seeds, and per-question logs make the work reproducible
Router composition recipe (TTMG-Abstain / TTMG+R-LLM) shows how to preserve gains while matching flat-RAG overall accuracy
weaknesses: n=150 stratified slice is tiny; confidence intervals overlap heavily and McNemar p-values hover >0.05 for most slices
Intent classifier recall on multi-session only 42 %—router leaves ~15 pp on the table vs oracle
Writer under-extracts assistant claims (7/7 SSA errors) and multi-claim arithmetic fails (8/8 MS errors); these are structural
Hard-edge threshold τ_hard=0.7 chosen without sensitivity analysis; no ablation on τ_sim or k_keep
No latency breakdown for linker vs reader; end-to-end 85 s still too slow for interactive use
Evaluation limited to one English benchmark; no cross-domain or multilingual test
required_revisions: Run on full LongMemEval-S (n=500) and report stratified bootstrap CIs; include power calculation for n=150
Add τ_hard, τ_sim, k_keep grid search and show slice accuracy vs latency/token trade-off curve
Quantify linker false-positive rate and its cost in abstention over-triggering; release edge label confusion matrix
Implement assistant-side claim densification and multi-claim reader prompt; report regain on SSA/MS slices
Break down per-component latency (writer/linker/reader) and target ≤1 s interactive budget
Evaluate on LoCoMo or MemoryAgentBench to demonstrate generalisation beyond LongMemEval

strengths: Novel synthesis of classical truth maintenance with modern LLM agent memory
Claim-level schema with validity intervals and explicit contradict/supersede edges is principled
Comprehensive ablation isolating the LLM linker's causal contribution
Honest reporting of regressions and detailed failure mode taxonomy (F1-F3)
Efficiency gains over A-Mem baseline (-62.1% tokens)
Well-written with clear method description, algorithm pseudocode, and full prompt appendix
weaknesses: Main capability claims rely on very small samples (n=9 abstention, n=21 SSU)
McNemar p=0.0625 for abstention is not significant at α=0.05
Overall accuracy regresses from 68.0% to 61.3% (even if not statistically significant)
Router compositions that recover overall accuracy mostly fall back to Flat RAG, limiting practical novelty
Intent classifier has poor recall on MS (42.5%) and KU (39.1%) slices
No comparison to more recent memory systems beyond A-Mem
required_revisions: Expand evaluation to full LongMemEval-S (500 questions) or report power analysis justifying N=150
Report effect sizes alongside p-values for small-n slices
Clarify whether the 95% CIs overlap meaningfully between TTMG and Flat RAG overall
Add comparison to at least one additional recent memory baseline (e.g., MemGPT, MemoryBank)
Discuss deployment implications when router sends most traffic to Flat RAG

strengths: Novel application of truth maintenance (validity intervals, contradict/supersede edges) to agent memory
Good failure-mode analysis (F1-F3) explaining regressions on SSA, SSP, MS slices
Comprehensive ablation showing linker's causal role across all slices
Strong reproducibility commitments: full prompts, hyperparameters, seed, promise to release code and raw outputs
Efficiency gains over A-Mem baseline (-62.1% tokens) with practical deployment implications
Honest reporting of non-significant overall difference (McNemar p=0.21)
weaknesses: Abstention claim (n=9) has p=0.0625 one-sided, NOT significant at α=0.05; two-sided p=0.125
SSU claim (n=21) has McNemar p=0.50, NOT statistically significant despite being presented as a 'win'
Sample sizes too small for reliable inference: abstention n=9, KU n=23, SSU n=21
Router compositions (TTMG-Abstain, TTMG+R-LLM) show abstention accuracy of 44.4%, same as Flat RAG, contradicting claim that they 'retain TTMG's truth-maintenance behaviours'
Only one ablation (--contradict); no ablations of claim schema alone, validity intervals alone, or MCS algorithm
Overall accuracy regresses from 68.0% to 61.3% (not significant, but directionally negative)
required_revisions: Run experiments on larger sample sizes, especially for abstention slice (current n=9 is underpowered); consider using full LongMemEval-S (500 questions)
Report two-sided p-values for all tests; one-sided p=0.0625 should not be claimed as significant
Clarify why TTMG-Abstain and TTMG+R-LLM routers show 44.4% abstention accuracy (same as Flat RAG) if they preserve TTMG's abstention behavior
Add ablations isolating individual components: claim schema without linker, validity intervals alone, MCS algorithm vs simpler alternatives
Tone down claims of 'paired set-inclusion wins' when p-values are not significant (SSU p=0.50)
Report effect sizes with confidence intervals, not just point estimates and p-values

strengths: Novel claim-level memory schema with explicit truth-maintenance edges (contradict, supersede)
Practical inference-time routing (TTMG-Abstain, TTMG+R-LLM) requiring no benchmark labels
Significant efficiency gains: -62.1% tokens vs A-Mem, 85s vs 117.1s latency
Strong ablation study isolating linker's causal role with per-slice breakdown
Clear failure mode taxonomy (F1-F3) motivating router composition design
Well-documented prompts, hyperparameters, and reproducibility statement
weaknesses: Very small sample sizes on key slices: n=9 abstention, n=21 SSU limit statistical power
Standalone TTMG regresses on SSA (-35.3pp), SSP (-33.4pp), MS (-7.5pp) slices
Overall accuracy 61.3% vs Flat RAG 68.0% (though p=0.21, not significant)
Intent classifier has poor recall on MS (42.5%) and KU (39.1%), limiting router effectiveness
Single benchmark evaluation (LongMemEval-S only); no comparison to MemGPT, MemoryBank
Abstention McNemar p=0.0625 one-sided is borderline; two-sided p=0.125 not significant
required_revisions: Expand evaluation to full 500-question LongMemEval-S or add LoCoMo/MemoryAgentBench
Report per-component latency breakdown (writer, linker, retriever, reader) for production planning
Analyze sensitivity to key hyperparameters (similarity gate 0.65, hard-edge threshold 0.7)
Add comparison to at least one additional memory baseline (MemGPT or MemoryBank)
Address intent classifier weakness on MS/KU slices or quantify impact on router performance
Report memory footprint and index size for deployment planning
